scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

Ram Kumar,
S/o {{FATHER'S_NAME}},
Aged about 45 years,
R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – 560041.      ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o {{FATHER'S_NAME}},
Aged about 38 years,
R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru,
Karnataka – 560034.      ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The above-named Plaintiff most respectfully submits as under:

SECTION: COURT FEE

1. The Plaintiff pays court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on the suit value of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

SECTION: VALUATION

2. For the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only).

SECTION: PARTIES

3. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is engaged in lawful business and profession. The Plaintiff is a person competent to sue and be sued.

4. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is a person within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The Defendant is competent to be sued.

SECTION: FACTS

5. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant at his request and on his promise to repay the same on demand.

6. The said amount was transferred by the Plaintiff to the Defendant through NEFT bank transfer from the Plaintiff's bank account, bearing UTR No. {{UTR_NUMBER}}, on 15.03.2024.

7. There was no written agreement between the parties regarding the said loan. However, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages sent from his mobile to the Plaintiff.

8. The Defendant promised to repay the loan amount on demand. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands to the Defendant for repayment.

9. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the said oral demands. The date of default is 20.06.2024.

10. The Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be sent to the Defendant through the Plaintiff's advocate, calling upon the Defendant to repay the loan amount along with interest.

11. Despite service of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount or any part thereof. The Defendant has thus wrongfully retained the Plaintiff's money.

SECTION: COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT COMPLIANCE

12. The Plaintiff states that the present suit is filed in compliance with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The Plaintiff has exhausted the remedy of pre-institution mediation or claims exemption as applicable under the law.

SECTION: STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

13. The Statement of Account showing the principal amount, interest computation, and balance due is as under:

| Sl. No. | Particulars | Amount (Rs.) |
|---------|-------------|--------------|
| 1. | Principal loan amount advanced on 15.03.2024 | 15,00,000.00 |
| 2. | Part-payments received | Nil |
| 3. | Balance principal due | 15,00,000.00 |
| 4. | Interest @ 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 to {{DATE}} (195 days) | 96,000.00 |
| 5. | Total amount due as on {{DATE}} | 15,96,000.00 |

SECTION: CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024, being the date of default, when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the oral demands made by the Plaintiff.

15. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024, when the Plaintiff caused a legal notice to be issued to the Defendant demanding repayment, and the Defendant failed to comply.

16. The cause of action is continuing and subsists as the Defendant has not repaid the loan amount till date. The suit is within limitation as per Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years for suits for money payable by the defendant to the plaintiff.

SECTION: LEGAL BASIS

17. Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 provides that the Court shall have jurisdiction to try all suits of a civil nature excepting suits of which its cognizance is either expressly or impliedly barred. The present suit is a civil suit for recovery of money and is maintainable before this Hon'ble Court.

18. Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 provides that every suit shall be instituted in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the defendant actually and voluntarily resides, or carries on business, or works for gain, or where the cause of action wholly or in part arises. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action has also arisen within the said jurisdiction.

19. Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation from the party who has broken the contract for any loss or damage caused to him thereby. The Defendant's failure to repay the loan amounts to breach of the contract, entitling the P
